channel.title: Blogs on Himanshu Sharma's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Blogs on Himanshu Sharma's Blog

item.title: Recommendation Systems
item.description:
There are three core algorithms behind recommendation systems:

  1. Collaborative Filtering
  2. Content-Based Filtering
  3. Hybrid Approach Filtering

Collaborative Filtering

Collaborative filtering algorithm attempts to predict what a user will like based on the patterns from other users, hence the name. It does not factor in the item content.

The advantage of collaborative filtering is that it can find more hidden patterns.

Why does collaborative filtering able to find hidden patterns better? Collaborative filtering works better because of co-occurrence. (Co-occurrence is basically two events occurring more when together) This allows for subconscious patterns and nuances to be recognized by the model that typical genres and classes cannot analyze.

Even within collaborative filtering we have more approaches.
